zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Stewart Countess of Argyll於1459年4月9日出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾達利時，她的父親Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Lennox33歲，母親Margaret Montgomery Countess of Lennox35歲。她和Archibald Campbell, 2nd Earl of Argyll至少有3個兒子和6個女兒。她去世於1529年12月9日在蘇格蘭可拉可曼南史爾多拉亞享年70歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭雷福福爾雷福盧。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Stewart Countess of Argyll was born on 9 April 1459, in Darnley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, her father, Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Lennox, was 33 and her mother, Margaret Montgomery Countess of Lennox, was 35. She had at least 3 sons and 6 daughters with Archibald Campbell, 2nd Earl of Argyll. She died on 9 December 1529, in Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, at the age of 70, and was buried in Renfrew, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Stewart Countess of Argyll est née le 9 avril 1459, à Darnley, Renfrewshire, Écosse, son père, Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Lennox, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Margaret Montgomery Countess of Lennox, avait 35 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles avec Archibald Campbell, 2nd Earl of Argyll. Elle est décédée le 9 décembre 1529, à Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Renfrew, Renfrewshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Elizabeth Stewart Countess of Argyll am 9. April 1459 in Darnley, Renfrewshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Lennox 33 und ihre Mutter Margaret Montgomery Countess of Lennox 35. Sie hatte mit Archibald Campbell, 2nd Earl of Argyll mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 9. Dezember 1529 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Scotland und wurde in Renfrew, Renfrewshire, Scotland begraben.

Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie con Archibald Campbell, 2nd Earl of Argyll. Morì il 9 dicembre 1529, nel Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolta a Renfrew, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Stewart Countess of Argyllが1459年4月9日にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアダルンリーで生まれた時，父Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Lennoxは33歳，母Margaret Montgomery Countess of Lennoxは35歳であった。彼女はArchibald Campbell, 2nd Earl of Argyllとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1529年12月9日にスコットランドクラクマナンシュアイアドラーで70歳で死去し，スコットランドレンフリューシュアイアレンフリューにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Stewart Countess of Argyll(이)가 1459년 4월 9일 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 더른리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Lennox(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Montgomery Countess of Lennox(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 Archibald Campbell, 2nd Earl of Argyll(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1529년 12월 9일 스컷랜드 클랙먼아실르 돌라에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 렌프레에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Stewart Countess of Argyll родилась 9 апреля 1459 г. в местности дарнли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Lennox было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Montgomery Countess of Lennox, 35. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей от Archibald Campbell, 2nd Earl of Argyll. Она умерла 9 декабря 1529 г. в местности доллар, клаккманнаньшир, Шотландия в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности ренфрю, ренфрюшир, Шотландия.
